validation_output.command: cd desktop && bun run test -- generalSettings
validation_output.summary: RED as expected: 52 tests ran, 48 passed, 4 failed. The passing new default-export test proves the normal provider export calls exportProviders, renders containsSecrets false, and does not include the fixture secret. The 4 failing RED tests all fail because the Settings UI has no accessible 'Export with credentials' action yet, so warning, second confirmation, cancel/no-export, and non-remembered confirmation behavior are missing.

must_preserve_evidence.G-SECRET-EXPORT: Tests require a separate credential-bearing export action and assert default export remains secret-free and does not call exportProvidersWithSecrets.
must_preserve_evidence.G-TEST: Added focused Vitest/Testing Library tests only; ran the requested focused test command and captured nonzero RED output.
must_preserve_evidence.G-SCOPE: Only edited desktop/src/__tests__/generalSettings.test.tsx; no production, server, env, credential, or user config files were edited.
must_preserve_evidence.MP-003: Tests preserve both paths by proving default secret-free export and specifying a separate credential-bearing export path.
must_preserve_evidence.MP-005: Tests require high-friction warning text, an explicit acknowledgment checkbox, cancellation without export, and confirmation reset across reopen.
must_preserve_evidence.CA-007: Tests assert confirmation payload { acknowledgedCredentialExposure: true }, no export without confirmation/cancel, and no remembered confirmation state.

concerns: The repository already has many unrelated modified/untracked files, including other provider import/export implementation files. I did not revert or overwrite them.

The assigned test file already contained concurrent provider import/export test edits before this task; I preserved them and added the credential-export RED coverage in the same Providers describe block.
recovery_hints: Implement a separate Settings Providers action with accessible name matching /Export with credentials/i.
Add a warning dialog named /Export providers with credentials/i with the tested warning copy and acknowledgment checkbox.
Wire the confirmed action to providerStore.exportProvidersWithSecrets({ all/providerIds, confirmation: { acknowledgedCredentialExposure: true } }) without calling the normal export action.
Reset the acknowledgment state whenever the warning dialog is opened.
